blocks.content: November. The cold in Minneapolis became a different thing entirely — not the cold Daisy had been warned about but the actual thing, the kind that had weight and intent. She bought a second coat. And a third.
Study group reassembled, minus any extra conversations. Jordan had taken the no cleanly. He was professional about it, which she respected. The group worked. She wrote.
Her lit professor handed back her midterm paper with a note at the top in red ink: The best argument in this class this semester. She took a photo and sent it without a caption.
framing that and putting it on my wall
i have the photo. close enough
claire got us hot chocolate from the place on washington

They were almost back. The careful texts had given way to normal ones, then to longer ones, and something in the fight had burned off the last of whatever they'd both been holding at arm's length. They'd had it out. They were still here.
i'm kidding. thank you kit. really
